pi_items.source: Web search of Enron corpus (enron-mail.com, academic databases); no matching email found with this exact subject line
Jeffrey Skilling, CEO of Enron, resigned on August 14, 2001 (verified via Bloomberg, CNN, SEC documents)
Full name confirmed as Jeffrey K. Skilling in SEC enforcement documents and news sources
Kenneth Lay was CEO/Chairman who received Skilling's resignation notice on July 13, 2001, and resumed CEO role on August 14 (verified via Bloomberg, SEC, congressional records)
Full name confirmed as Kenneth L. Lay in official SEC documents and SEC enforcement litigation releases
Official announcement date of Skilling's resignation as Enron CEO (verified via Bloomberg, CNN, SEC documents, Oil & Gas Journal)

Enron stock closed down $2.68 (6.2%) at $40.25/share on August 14, 2001. Skilling told Lay on July 13 he was resigning because he felt unable to stop stock decline (verified via Oil & Gas Journal, CNN, Bloomberg)

privileged_info_summary: Jeffrey Skilling resigned from Enron as CEO on August 14, 2001, and notified Kenneth Lay of this decision. The resignation had significant stock impact, with Enron shares falling 6.2% to $40.25 on the announcement date. Skilling had privately informed Lay on July 13, 2001 that he was resigning because he felt unable to stop the decline in Enron's stock price. The subject line 'Resignation implications' could not be verified in the publicly available Enron email corpus or academic sources.
